steps.action: Eskista (Amharic: እስክስታ) is a traditional Amhara with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Eskista (Amharic: እስክስታ) is a traditional Amhara with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: Due to the widespread influence of the Amhara pe
steps.text: Due to the widespread influence of the Amhara people, the dance is performed in almost every corner of the country and has gone viral internationally, becoming a global trend celebrated by diverse audiences.

steps.action: The dance is characterized by its rapid, rhythmi
steps.text: The dance is characterized by its rapid, rhythmic movements of the upper body, particularly the shoulders, but also the chest, head, and neck.

steps.action: Eskista is typically performed to traditional Am
steps.text: Eskista is typically performed to traditional Amhara music, but it is possible to incorporate the style of dance into modern forms of music such as the music played in modern Ethiopian music videos.

steps.action: The complex nature of eskista dancing is what ma
steps.text: The complex nature of eskista dancing is what makes it arguably one of the most technical forms of African traditional dance.

steps.action: The dance resembles and lis likely related to th
steps.text: The dance resembles and lis likely related to the shimshim of the Tigrayan-Tigrinya people in northern Ethiopia and Eritrea as well as the Beja Hadendoa traditional dance.

steps.action: Like many traditional African dances, Eskista is
steps.text: Like many traditional African dances, Eskista is often accompanied by the practice of awarding money, known as shilimat (Amharic: ሽለማት), to the best dancer.

steps.action: Spectators typically stick the money onto the da
steps.text: Spectators typically stick the money onto the dancer's forehead as a sign of admiration and encouragement, adding a festive and interactive element to the performance.

steps.action: It is enjoyed by men, women, and children.
steps.text: It is enjoyed by men, women, and children.

steps.action: Eskista is a common sight at weddings, celebrati
steps.text: Eskista is a common sight at weddings, celebrations, and gatherings, serving as a vibrant expression of joy and communal bonding.

steps.action: This tradition highlights the cultural significa
steps.text: This tradition highlights the cultural significance of eskista as both an art form and a means of fostering social connection and celebration.
